name: S&P 500
summary: At 6,528.52 (+2.91%) — best day since May on Pezeshkian peace signal. VIX below 30 (25.25) = algo selling stops. IRGC threatens 18 US companies with deadline tonight — binary risk event. Rally built on rhetoric, not confirmed action. March saw multiple relief-crash cycles (March 23→28→30). Still -5.1% monthly. CRITICAL RISK: IRGC corporate threat deadline 20:00 Tehran. If executed, VIX back above 30 = cascade.

price_snapshots.trigger: Largest single-session rally since May — Pezeshkian peace signal
price_snapshots.delta_context: 441 of 500 components green. VIX collapsed -17.5% to 25.25 (below 30 threshold). NASDAQ +3.83%, Dow +2.49%. Defense/shipping soft. SMH +5.8%. IRGC corporate threat deadline tonight = binary risk event.

signals.content: Energy and defense stocks surged: Lockheed +15%, Exxon +5%+, Northrop +6%. Investors aggressively rotating from tech/growth into defense and energy.
S&P 500 dropped 1.5%, Nasdaq 100 plunged 1.8% to six-month lows. Correction territory. Stocks, bonds, and gold all selling off simultaneously.

S&P 500 soared 2.2% (+143 pts) to 6,605 after Trump announced 5-day strike pause. Major relief rally. US markets reacted to de-escalation signal; Asian markets had already sold off on the earlier escalation threats (time-zone divergence).

S&P 500 at 6,369 (-7.8% monthly, -1.7% daily). VIX surged 13.2% to 31.05 — crossing 30 triggers institutional risk-off (algo selling, portfolio de-risking). Breakdown: ~55% crisis-driven (Houthi entry, Saudi base attack, Tel Aviv strike, regional bombardment), ~30% tariff-driven (Section 122 tariffs 10-15% global, Yale Budget Lab: +0.5-0.6% price level, +0.3pp unemployment), ~15% other. VIX above 30 creates self-reinforcing cycle: VIX up → algos sell equities → equities drop → VIX higher.

S&P 500 at 6,343.72 (-0.4%, -7.8% monthly). VIX dropped below 30 to 28.61 (-6.5%) on Trump 'willing to end war' headlines — technically significant as VIX >30 triggers institutional algo selling. SMH (semiconductors) -3.1% on TurboQuant + helium. Defense (ITA) -2.4% on peace headlines + Boeing NGAD + EU EDIP. KOSPI -4.3% is a leading indicator of semiconductor supply chain stress. Market pricing 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end (first time >50%). Gold regime shift, if confirmed, extends equity drawdown window. CONFIRMED.

S&P surged +2.91% to 6,528.52 — best day since May. Triggered by Iran's Pezeshkian telling EU he has 'necessary will' to end war. 441 of 500 components green. VIX collapsed -17.5% to 25.25 (below 30 institutional threshold — algo selling stops). NASDAQ +3.83% (Nvidia +5%, Meta +6%), Dow +2.49%. SMH +5.8% on helium relief. Defense/shipping soft (ITA flat, BOAT -0.14%, Frontline -0.77%). DXY dropped below 100 to 99.79. HOWEVER: IRGC threatened 18 named US companies (Apple, Google, Nvidia, JPMorgan) with deadline tonight 20:00 Tehran — if executed, VIX spikes back above 30 triggering algo selling cascade. Rally built on REPORTED peace signals, not confirmed de-escalation — same pattern that produced March 23 crash reversal. CONTRADICTORY: oil ALSO rose intraday to $118.60 before settling at $105 — Hormuz decoupling thesis. (Sources: Yahoo Finance, CNBC, Bloomberg)

edges.context: Primary US equity benchmark. Reflecting war costs, inflation fears, and Fed uncertainty.
Market pricing 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end (first time >50%). Higher discount rates compress valuations. Fed trapped — can't cut or hike.
Oil spike driving cost inflation and margin compression. Gold regime shift extending equity drawdown window. Energy benefits but tech-heavy index suffers net.
SMH +5.8% on helium relief from Pezeshkian peace signal. KOSPI +6.4% leading indicator. Qatar Ras Laffan = 30-38% global helium removed. Fabs still on 50% allocations.

trigger_points.condition: S&P 500 enters bear market (down 20% from peak)
trigger_points.mechanism: Would force Fed to consider emergency support despite inflation. Would crash global markets including India. Could paradoxically lead to war de-escalation pressure.
